edges.label: POST /invoke or POST /messages
edges.notes: Accept: application/json (or absent) for /invoke; Accept: text/event-stream for streaming /messages.

edges.label: dispatch to the agent workflow handler (_agent)
edges.notes: In-process dispatch inside the same 'services' container; not a network hop.

edges.label: /messages only: mint/validate session_id, fold UIMessage[] to neutral Message[]
edges.notes: /invoke bypasses this adapter entirely and reads data.inputs.messages directly.

edges.label: resolve_tools(agent_config.tools), resolve_mcp_servers(...)
edges.notes: In-process SDK call (not a network hop by itself); resolution then makes its own HTTP calls out to agenta-api.

edges.label: POST /tools/resolve (gateway), POST /secrets/resolve (named secrets)
edges.notes: Gateway resolution validates the connection and enriches from the Composio catalog; reference/platform tools resolve with no HTTP round-trip.

edges.label: POST /run (HTTP to :8765) or CLI subprocess on stdin
edges.notes: HTTP when AGENTA_RUNNER_INTERNAL_URL is set (deployed containers); CLI subprocess transport otherwise (source checkout). Same AgentRunRequest payload either way. Streaming uses Accept: application/x-ndjson / --stream.

edges.label: AgentRunResult (batch) or NDJSON stream terminated by one result record
edges.notes: ok:false raises in Python. On the streaming path the terminal result's events array is empty; events already went out live.

edges.label: live NDJSON events -> neutral AgentEvent objects
edges.notes: The Python AgentRun turns {"kind":"event"} records into live AgentEvent objects as they arrive.

edges.label: Vercel UI Message Stream framed as SSE
edges.notes: First part carries messageMetadata.sessionId; stream ends with data: [DONE].

edges.label: start the sandbox-agent daemon for this /run
edges.notes: Cold: one daemon process per turn, torn down in the finally path once the turn ends.

edges.label: resolve harness id 'pi' -> start pi-acp
edges.notes: Chosen when the request's harness field is pi_core or pi_agenta.

edges.label: resolve harness id 'claude' -> start claude-agent-acp
edges.notes: Chosen when the request's harness field is claude.

edges.label: spawn the pi CLI from PATH
edges.notes: pi-acp does not bundle Pi; the runner points it at a pinned pi binary (PI_ACP_PI_COMMAND).

edges.label: spawn the claude CLI (downloaded over HTTPS on first use)
edges.notes: Requires ca-certificates in the sidecar image; auth via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN.

edges.label: load the bundled extension into Pi's agent directory
edges.notes: Pi loads the extension every run; it registers tools and hooks tracing from inside the Pi process.

edges.label: POST /tools/call directly (local sandbox only)
edges.notes: On a local sandbox the extension runs on the runner host, so each registered tool's body posts straight back to Agenta with the call_ref and the model's arguments.

edges.label: tool-call request file, and (pi-builtin-gating) permission record
edges.notes: Used for every Daytona tool call, and on any sandbox for the tool_call hook that reports each Pi builtin invocation as a permission record (kind: "permission").

edges.label: call the agenta-tools MCP server over loopback HTTP
edges.notes: Attached to the ACP session as a type:"http" MCP server; reachable only from loopback.

edges.label: relay the call back to the runner (file relay, used even on local runs)
edges.notes: The bridge holds only public metadata; the runner executes the private spec from memory.

edges.label: POST /tools/call (callback/reference), or a direct call (platform ops)
edges.notes: A resolved spec carries call_ref (routes through /tools/call) XOR call (the runner calls the endpoint directly, reusing toolCallback.authorization).

edges.label: execute the resolved Composio action
edges.notes: Only reached for gateway (type: "gateway") tools. The Composio key never leaves agenta-api.

edges.label: decide(gate, plan, storedDecisions) before executing
edges.notes: In-process function call. Enforced for Pi (gateway, code, and now builtins); Claude's own gates decide before a call reaches the relay, so RelayPermissions.enforce is false there.

edges.label: session.onPermissionRequest (a gate Claude raises itself, undecided by its own settings.json)
edges.notes: Claude checks its rendered .claude/settings.json first; only an undecided call reaches this responder.

edges.label: decide(gate, plan, storedDecisions)
edges.notes: Same shared decision function the tool relay calls, so both gates can never disagree about a tool's permission.

edges.label: reply "once" (allow this call only) or "reject"
edges.notes: allow maps to "once", never "always" -- every call is gated individually, never broadly pre-approved.

edges.label: create a Daytona VM; push login, Pi extension, AGENTS.md, skills, system-prompt files
edges.notes: Over the Daytona filesystem API. One sandbox per turn, ephemeral, auto-stopped after SANDBOX_AGENT_DAYTONA_AUTOSTOP_MINUTES (default 15) as a leak backstop.

edges.label: in-sandbox tool child writes *.req.json, polls for *.res.json over the sandbox filesystem API
edges.notes: The runner's list()/read()/write() use sandbox.runProcess("ls") and readFsFile/writeFsFile on Daytona, vs. plain node:fs on local.

edges.label: self-instrumented spans: invoke_agent (AGENT) > turn N (CHAIN) > chat (LLM) + execute_tool (TOOL)
edges.notes: Started as a child of AGENTA_TRACEPARENT, so the whole Pi run joins the caller's /invoke trace. Also writes usage totals to AGENTA_USAGE_OUT on agent_end.

edges.label: spans built from the ACP event stream (Claude, and any non-self-instrumenting harness)
edges.notes: Same span tree shape as Pi's, but built by the runner subscribing to session/update rather than an in-process extension.

edges.label: fill context.propagation/telemetry from the live workflow span; roll usage back onto it
edges.notes: tracing.py's trace_context()/run_context() supply the /run request's context and runContext blocks; the same module stamps the returned usage on the workflow span.

edges.label: hold an InMemorySessionPersistDriver for the life of this /run process
edges.notes: Never survives past the one /run call; not a warm session store.

edges.label: select AgentaHarness for harness=pi_agenta
edges.notes: Runs on the same sandbox-agent/pi ACP path as pi_core; the runner has no separate engine for it.

edges.label: (not selected in the deployed stack) would create_sandbox/create_session
edges.notes: select_backend always constructs SandboxAgentBackend regardless of harness, so this edge is never actually exercised today; shown for completeness of the SDK's Backend port.
